# Release Notes {.unnumbered}

This course site is a living product. It gets better during the semester, and every meaningful update ships as a numbered release so you can see exactly what changed and when. The current version is shown in the footer of every page.

New entries appear at the top.

## v1.2.0 (2026-07-20)

A large release: a new Product Strategy chapter, the course reorganized around the AIRE loop, two new reference pages, and the schedule and assessments brought in line with the new structure.

- **New chapter, Product Strategy** (in the Aim part): what game you are playing, where to play and how to win, moats, and the DHM test for a strong strategy.
- **The course is now organized into five parts around the AIRE loop:** Foundations, Aim, Discover, Build, Grow, and a References section. Product Morality now sits in Aim (what is worth building), and Platform Strategy moves into Build.
- **Chapter 1 adds a table** showing how the AIRE loop (Aim, Inform, Reason, Enact) maps to the parts of the course.
- **Two new reference pages:** The Canon, which credits the sources behind each major principle and flags what has changed in the AI era, and a Thought Leaders page.
- **Attribution pass across the course:** principles are now credited to their sources, with new frameworks from Jeff Dyer added to Problems Worth Solving, Building Your MVP, Go-to-Market, and Storytelling.
- **Schedule:** the weekly practice table is rebuilt around the new parts. Product Strategy gets its own practice, and the readings and quiz schedule is updated. There are still ten quizzes; three weeks now pair two shorter chapters under one quiz.
- **Assessments:** the quiz-to-chapter mapping matches the new chapters, and all sprint, peer, final, and survey dates are corrected to the Fall 2026 calendar.

## v1.1.2 (2026-07-18)

New discovery and MVP tools, adapted with credit from Nile Hatch's *Expeditionary Innovation*.

- **Problems Worth Solving:** a "diverge before you converge" section on generating many candidate problems before narrowing.
- **Problem Discovery:** the five kinds of pain (physical, functional, financial, emotional, social) as a probing checklist, and a "how many interviews is enough?" note on interviewing to saturation.
- **Validating Opportunities:** the Access Test (can you actually reach these customers?) and a "harden your evidence" note on willingness-to-pay, the Ouch Factor, and false positives.
- **Building Your MVP:** the $100 Test for feature prioritization, and how to test demand before building (smoke test and Wizard of Oz).

## v1.1.1 (2026-07-18)

Small additions to the discovery and storytelling material.

- **Chapter 3** now has a "thinking in probabilities" note: hold beliefs about your customer as probabilities and seek evidence that would look different if you were wrong, which is why the Mom Test works.
- **A rider-and-elephant illustration** was added to the Storytelling chapter and to Product Morality, to picture the two-part mind the analogy describes.

## v1.1.0 (2026-07-18)

A large content release: a new chapter, a framework that ties the whole course together, and expanded discovery, storytelling, and grading material.

- **New chapter, Product Morality** (in Grow the Business): how to aim a product well, moral foundations, a reflection test for habit-forming products, and Aristotle's golden mean with a virtue table.
- **Chapter 1 now opens with "The Loop: How This Course Works":** the AIRE loop the course runs on (Aim, Inform, Reason, Enact), why the decision factors multiply, and how each class runs as a practice.
- **Chapter 3 now opens with "Discovery Is Truth-Seeking":** a belief-and-truth map with a new diagram, the six ways of knowing and why you triangulate, and how much evidence is enough before you commit.
- **Storytelling and the Final Demo is now a full chapter:** building a story database, techniques that make a story land, and a three-part structure for the final video.
- **New Playbook page, "Why This Class Works This Way,"** explaining the course design, including why the class is graded on decisions and learning rather than outcomes.
- **Truth-seeking callbacks** added to Validating Opportunities, Testing with Users, and Measuring What Matters.
- **Schedule:** scrimmages (Practices 11 and 12) are now described; Wednesday office hours; updated TA and course-number information.
- **Navigation reorganized** into solo-builder phases: Foundations, Discover, Build, Grow, Demo, Playbook.
- **Overview and Syllabus:** refreshed grading summary, cross-listed course numbers, and AI-tool requirements updated to the Claude Max plan.
- Fixes and polish: resources updated, version chip restyled.

## v1.0.0 (2026-07-18)

The course site is now versioned. From here on, every published update to the schedule, assignments, or course content will be listed on this page with a version number and date.

- Added this release notes page, linked from the version number in the site footer.
- Established the initial baseline: course information, schedule, assessments, twelve topic chapters, and resources.
